action.reasoning: Observation: v16 heuristic still 0 safe_steps (max streak=0), high avg_dist~6m, min_d>2.2m, variable detector (0-110/ep), 0 collisions. Config confirms 500 accumulated steps in [1.0,2.0)m with |angle|<0.2618rad (~15deg) for success; facing_threshold=0.5. Test ran full but policy fails to enter+maintain safe state vs dynamic oracle target. Earlier v4-v5 hit ~50-100 safe_steps. Analysis: follow mode triggers rarely (detector low), OA/depth patches miss human-as-obstacle cases, speed-matching (0.32) + angular gain insufficient for persistent facing while human moves randomly; pure-rotate threshold too loose. Root cause: stabilization logic not robust enough for accumulation. Literature heuristic (SPF) hard due to navmesh/dynamic goal in this task. Decision: rewrite COMPLETE non-truncated test_full_episode.py + predict.py with clean v17 HeuristicPolicy (detector>0.4 prioritizes stable follow at 0.25m/s speed-match + stronger -8*angle facing + pure-rotate if |angle|>0.25; reduced OA sensitivity only on detector<0.2+min_front<1.0 with corrected left/right bias; aggressive seek linear=0.85 when far; backup <0.9m; refined depth (valid>0.1, better center 40% patch); manual safe count using exact success_angle+dist+metrics fallback; expanded per-epi print (safe, streak, min_d, detector%, collisions, final_metrics); full JSON contract with make_serializable, per_episode dicts using env.get_metrics() + manual safe_follow_steps, output dir creation). Update train.py to v17. Run test_full_episode.py (5 eps) for validation of improved safe_steps/streak/detector before submit_eval.
